hotspots.title: Hungary’s regime change under Péter Magyar
hotspots.blurb: Hungary's new parliament convened, electing Péter Magyar as prime minister after his Tisza party won a historic two-thirds majority in April 2026 elections, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule. Magyar's inauguration marks a significant shift in Hungarian politics, with promises of systemic change and anti-corruption measures.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the historic nature of the transition, the emotional framing around the end of Orbán's era, and the moral charge of Magyar's anti-corruption platform.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s historic local election surge
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK made unprecedented gains in the UK's 2026 local elections, winning over 1,400 council seats and taking control of multiple councils, including Essex, Sunderland, and Havering. The party's success signals a major shift in British politics, challenging Labour and Conservative dominance.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the historic scale of Reform UK's gains, the emotional framing of a political earthquake, and the polarized contention over immigration and national identity.

hotspots.title: Manuel Adorni’s illicit enrichment scandal
hotspots.blurb: Argentina's Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni is under investigation for alleged illicit enrichment after a contractor claimed Adorni paid $245,000 in cash for home renovations. The scandal has sparked internal government tensions, with President Javier Milei publicly defending Adorni despite growing criticism.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the moral charge of corruption allegations, the polarized contention within the government, and the alarmist framing around Adorni's unexplained wealth.

hotspots.title: Vox’s internal conflicts and electoral challenges
hotspots.blurb: Vox faces internal divisions and electoral setbacks in Spain's 2026 regional elections, particularly in Andalusia. The party's aggressive campaign tactics, including anti-immigration rhetoric and criticism of government policies, have sparked controversy and backlash, while its leadership struggles to maintain unity.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the polarized contention over immigration, the moral charge of Vox's rhetoric, and the alarmist framing around the party's electoral decline.

hotspots.title: Trump’s Iran war escalation and diplomacy
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration's escalating tensions with Iran, including military strikes and threats of further action, have dominated U.S. foreign policy coverage. The conflict has sparked diplomatic efforts, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio engaging in high-stakes negotiations, while Trump's rhetoric and actions draw both support and criticism.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the alarmist framing of war escalation, the polarized contention over Trump's foreign policy, and the moral charge of military action.
